title: BIBS Colour Studio Soother 2-Pack, BPA Free Dummy Pacifier, Round Nipple. Natural Rubber Latex, Made in Denmark, Size 2 (6-18 Months), Jasmine Blush
features: Introducing BIBS Studio Collection: Redefining modern baby products with trend-inspired designs. Empower your parenting style with quality and safety, without sacrificing on trendiness.
Recommended by midwifes to support natural breastfeeding.
100% free from BPA, PVC and phthalates. Produced in accordance with the requirements for safety, health and environment of EU Standard EN 1400+A2.
A history of more than 40 years, with Danish made baby products. Focusing on high quality products in an aesthetic design and inspiring to a colorful beginning

Studio Collection Merchant video A printed colour pacifier These exclusive designs make their debut on the Colour pacifier , available in size 1 and 2 , with a round nipple in natural rubber latex . Nostalgic floral print With precision in every detail, these nostalgic florals embrace joyful retro vibes with a sophisticated modern twist. Striped print Thanks to their simplicity and timeless appeal, stripes have been a fashion staple for generations. Whimsical floral print Featuring a lively palette of whimsical flowers in quirky shapes and sizes, these prints offer a playful perspective. Soothe in style BIBS Studio Collection BIBS is excited to introduce the STUDIO COLLECTION : A transformative new line that redefines the look and feel of parenting. The collection kicks off with six bold prints that creatively reimagine our beloved Colour Pacifiers , each offering a new perspective for the modern parenting journey. This approach to innovation , creativity , and self-expression comes without compromise, ensuring that every product upholds the high standards of quality, safety, and excellence . BIBS Studio Collection Prints that speak, pacifiers that soothe Crafted by the renowned French print house , Glitter Power Club, The STUDIO COLLECTION's blend current fashion trends with timeless appeal, offering a more aesthetic choice "I hope that parents will see these prints as something different and new. There's a shortage of cool-looking baby products in the market, which is unfortunate because it's really special to share your unique aesthetic with your child. It's another way to show how much you care." Naïma Jakimowicz, Glitter Power Club.
